title: ICE Hasn’t Reported Detention or Deportation Data in Months
excerpt: The agency blames government shutdowns for the delay, despite billions in new funding.
content_html:
The shooting deaths of two immigrants at the hands of Immigration and Customs Enforcement agents in the past week have brought the Trump administration’s violent crackdown into sharp relief once again, triggering protests and calls for accountability. But the government has kept the total number of people caught up in enforcement efforts under wraps since April, angering conservatives and progressives alike.

Dozens of immigrants have died in ICE detention since Trump took office. In recent days, two people, Lorenzo Salgado Araujo and Joan Sebastian Guerrero, have been fatally shot by ICE agents. In a midterm election year, experts say the need for transparency is urgent. Voices on the political right want proof the president is delivering on his promise to deport millions of people, while others on the left want to know how much damage has been done in immigrant communities.

“Even if there is a good reason for not releasing this information, it still gives the impression they are trying to hide something,” said Jessica Vaughan, director of policy studies for the Center for Immigration Studies, which advocates for restrictions on legal immigration. “They should be worried about giving that impression because they are already under so much scrutiny.”

ICE hasn’t released its semimonthly “Detention Management” report on arrests, detentions and deportations since April 9, when the agency reported holding more than 60,000 people in custody.

News media, think tanks and academia have requested the missing data, and ICE has refused to deliver it, sparking at least three separate lawsuits, including one by a far-right group demanding the “true deportation statistics.”

In an email, the Department of Homeland Security told The Marshall Project that, through June 24, the administration had arrested more than 981,000 people and deported 948,000 since Trump took office in January 2025.

An ICE spokesperson blamed a series of government shutdowns for what the agency described as a delay in publishing the data. “ICE is working diligently to the report [sic] and corresponding data out as soon as possible,” the spokesperson wrote in an email.

Months-long shutdowns have occasionally slowed the publication of data in the past, but “the government has been open for a while, and these reports should be routine,” Vaughan said.

Homeland Security also said “more than 3 million illegal aliens have left the U.S. … including an estimated 2.2 million self-deportations” in Trump’s first year back in office. The department has not said how it defines or tracks so-called self-deportations, or where the number came from.

“I think everybody’s aware that the numbers being thrown around via press releases and on social media don’t exactly check out,” said Mike Howell, director of the right-wing public policy nonprofit The Oversight Project, on a podcast with former Trump advisor Steve Bannon.

The Oversight Project sued the Trump administration for immigration enforcement data earlier this month, seeking records that back up the administration’s claims.

Howell’s start-up, an offshoot of the conservative Heritage Foundation, joins the list of news and nonprofit organizations seeking records.

The Deportation Data Project at UC Berkeley and UCLA, which publishes immigration data received from ICE, has also sued the administration; The Marshall Project has filed an amicus brief in support of the complaint. The lawsuit seeks records that “provide a quantitative window into how the U.S. government is carrying out immigration enforcement and how it compares to past administrations.”

The Deportation Data Project has become a go-to source for reporters and researchers, building on work compiling immigration data by groups like the Transactional Records Access Clearinghouse. It files Freedom of Information Act requests, sues when agencies stall and posts the raw files online for free, with tools to filter to a specific state or rough locality. Transparency advocates say this work shouldn’t absolve ICE of its duty to publicly report its data.

Since 2019, Congress has required ICE to publish detailed arrest, detention and deportation data in a report due every two weeks. The mandate was included in the 2020 Department of Homeland Security Appropriations Bill with bipartisan support.

The statute is explicit: The ICE director “shall make available a report, on a publicly accessible website in a downloadable, searchable, and sortable format,” updated semimonthly. In the report, ICE tracks and publishes detailed information on the state of immigration enforcement, including: the number of people detained, where they’re being held and for how long, and how many have been deported.

For the past six years, ICE has largely complied with the congressional data-reporting mandate. There were short delays on occasion, including last fall during a prolonged government shutdown — but the current gap is the longest, experts say.

“It’s important for us to know what our government is doing, especially in the Trump administration, which is known for its fabulism,” said Aaron Reichlin-Melnick, senior fellow at the American Immigration Council, a think tank that provides pro bono deportation defense and advocates for immigration reform.

Deportation flights, tracked independently by the nonprofit Human Rights First, continue to rise. But without official statistics, experts say, the full scope and consequences of the mass deportation program are impossible to know.

Because the transparency requirement was embedded in an appropriations bill, there is some debate about whether ICE is still legally required to report the data, Reichlin-Melnick said. The government has been operating since last year with funding approved under a process known as “reconciliation,” which Republicans could pass with a simple majority vote.

Under two separate reconciliation bills, Congress has given the Department of Homeland Security more than $240 billion for immigration enforcement through fiscal year 2029 — more money than the department has ever had. The first tranche of funding included $45 billion specifically for ICE detention.

For ICE to claim at any point since then that they were lacking funds “is not only disingenuous; it’s counter-factual,” said Heidi Altman, vice president of policy at the National Immigration Law Center, which advocates for the rights of low-income immigrants.

“As part of good governance, ICE should be reporting this basic data,” she said. “It shouldn’t require congressional instructions to do so.”

Other Homeland Security agencies have continued to provide timely information. Customs and Border Protection has updated its dashboard of migrant encounters through May, and ICE itself has continued to publish new 287g agreements through May.

Public data is “a form of accountability,” Vaughan said. “Voters are interested in this, and they’re going to be making decisions in many cases on the president’s record on immigration.”

blocks.html_en: The shooting deaths of two immigrants at the hands of Immigration and Customs Enforcement agents in the past week have brought the Trump administration’s violent crackdown into sharp relief once again, triggering protests and calls for accountability. But the government has kept the total number of people caught up in enforcement efforts under wraps since April, angering conservatives and progressives alike.

sentences.en: The shooting deaths of two immigrants at the hands of Immigration and Customs Enforcement agents in the past week have brought the Trump administration’s violent crackdown into sharp relief once again, triggering protests and calls for accountability.
sentences.zh: 过去一周发生的两名移民被移民和海关执法人员枪杀事件，让特朗普政府的暴力镇压行为再次暴露无遗，引发抗议和追究责任。

sentences.en: But the government has kept the total number of people caught up in enforcement efforts under wraps since April, angering conservatives and progressives alike.
sentences.zh: 但自四月份以来，政府一直对参与执法行动的总人数保密，这激怒了保守派和进步派。

blocks.html_en: Dozens of immigrants have died in ICE detention since Trump took office. In recent days, two people, Lorenzo Salgado Araujo and Joan Sebastian Guerrero, have been fatally shot by ICE agents. In a midterm election year, experts say the need for transparency is urgent. Voices on the political right want proof the president is delivering on his promise to deport millions of people, while others on the left want to know how much damage has been done in immigrant communities.

sentences.en: Dozens of immigrants have died in ICE detention since Trump took office.
sentences.zh: 自特朗普上任以来，已有数十名移民在 ICE 拘留期间死亡。

sentences.en: In recent days, two people, Lorenzo Salgado Araujo and Joan Sebastian Guerrero, have been fatally shot by ICE agents.
sentences.zh: 最近几天，Lorenzo Salgado Araujo 和 Joan Sebastian Guerrero 两人被 ICE 特工枪杀。

sentences.en: In a midterm election year, experts say the need for transparency is urgent.
sentences.zh: 专家表示，在中期选举年，迫切需要提高透明度。

sentences.en: Voices on the political right want proof the president is delivering on his promise to deport millions of people, while others on the left want to know how much damage has been done in immigrant communities.
sentences.zh: 政治右翼人士希望证明总统正在兑现驱逐数百万人的承诺，而左翼人士则想知道移民社区受到了多少损害。

blocks.html_en: “Even if there is a good reason for not releasing this information, it still gives the impression they are trying to hide something,” said Jessica Vaughan, director of policy studies for the Center for Immigration Studies, which advocates for restrictions on legal immigration. “They should be worried about giving that impression because they are already under so much scrutiny.”

sentences.en: “Even if there is a good reason for not releasing this information, it still gives the impression they are trying to hide something,” said Jessica Vaughan, director of policy studies for the Center for Immigration Studies, which advocates for restrictions on legal immigration.
sentences.zh: 主张限制合法移民的移民研究中心政策研究主任杰西卡·沃恩表示：“即使有充分的理由不公开这些信息，但这仍然给人留下他们试图隐瞒某些事情的印象。”

sentences.en: “They should be worried about giving that impression because they are already under so much scrutiny.”
sentences.zh: “他们应该担心给人留下这样的印象，因为他们已经受到如此多的审查。”

blocks.html_en: ICE hasn’t released its semimonthly “Detention Management” report on arrests, detentions and deportations since April 9, when the agency reported holding more than 60,000 people in custody.

sentences.en: ICE hasn’t released its semimonthly “Detention Management” report on arrests, detentions and deportations since April 9, when the agency reported holding more than 60,000 people in custody.
sentences.zh: 自 4 月 9 日该机构报告拘留了 6 万多人以来，ICE 就没有发布过关于逮捕、拘留和驱逐出境的半月刊“拘留管理”报告。

blocks.html_en: News media, think tanks and academia have requested the missing data, and ICE has refused to deliver it, sparking at least three separate lawsuits, including one by a far-right group demanding the “true deportation statistics.”

sentences.en: News media, think tanks and academia have requested the missing data, and ICE has refused to deliver it, sparking at least three separate lawsuits, including one by a far-right group demanding the “true deportation statistics.”
sentences.zh: 新闻媒体、智囊团和学术界已要求提供缺失的数据，但 ICE 拒绝提供这些数据，引发了至少三起不同的诉讼，其中包括一个极右翼组织发起的诉讼，要求提供“真实的驱逐统计数据”。

blocks.html_en: In an email, the Department of Homeland Security told The Marshall Project that, through June 24, the administration had arrested more than 981,000 people and deported 948,000 since Trump took office in January 2025.

sentences.en: In an email, the Department of Homeland Security told The Marshall Project that, through June 24, the administration had arrested more than 981,000 people and deported 948,000 since Trump took office in January 2025.
sentences.zh: 美国国土安全部在一封电子邮件中告诉《马歇尔计划》，自特朗普 2025 年 1 月上任以来，截至 6 月 24 日，政府已逮捕了超过 981,000 人，并驱逐了 948,000 人。

blocks.html_en: An ICE spokesperson blamed a series of government shutdowns for what the agency described as a delay in publishing the data. “ICE is working diligently to the report [sic] and corresponding data out as soon as possible,” the spokesperson wrote in an email.

sentences.en: An ICE spokesperson blamed a series of government shutdowns for what the agency described as a delay in publishing the data.
sentences.zh: ICE 发言人将数据发布的延迟归咎于一系列政府关闭。

sentences.en: “ICE is working diligently to the report [sic] and corresponding data out as soon as possible,” the spokesperson wrote in an email.
sentences.zh: 该发言人在一封电子邮件中写道：“ICE 正在努力研究该报告[原文如此]并尽快发布相应数据。”

blocks.html_en: Months-long shutdowns have occasionally slowed the publication of data in the past, but “the government has been open for a while, and these reports should be routine,” Vaughan said.

sentences.en: Months-long shutdowns have occasionally slowed the publication of data in the past, but “the government has been open for a while, and these reports should be routine,” Vaughan said.
sentences.zh: 过去长达数月的关闭有时会减慢数据的发布速度，但“政府已经开放了一段时间，这些报告应该是例行公事，”沃恩说。

blocks.html_en: Homeland Security also said “more than 3 million illegal aliens have left the U.S. … including an estimated 2.2 million self-deportations” in Trump’s first year back in office. The department has not said how it defines or tracks so-called self-deportations, or where the number came from.

sentences.en: Homeland Security also said “more than 3 million illegal aliens have left the U.S. …
sentences.zh: 国土安全部还表示，“超过 300 万非法移民已离开美国……

sentences.en: including an estimated 2.2 million self-deportations” in Trump’s first year back in office.
sentences.zh: 其中包括特朗普重新执政第一年估计有 220 万人自我驱逐。

sentences.en: The department has not said how it defines or tracks so-called self-deportations, or where the number came from.
sentences.zh: 该部门尚未透露如何定义或追踪所谓的自我驱逐出境，也没有说明该数字的来源。

blocks.html_en: “I think everybody’s aware that the numbers being thrown around via press releases and on social media don’t exactly check out,” said Mike Howell, director of the right-wing public policy nonprofit The Oversight Project, on a podcast with former Trump advisor Steve Bannon.

sentences.en: The lawsuit seeks records that “provide a quantitative window into how the U.S. government is carrying out immigration enforcement and how it compares to past administrations.”

sentences.en: The mandate was included in the 2020 Department of Homeland Security Appropriations Bill with bipartisan support.
sentences.zh: 该任务在两党支持下被纳入 2020 年国土安全部拨款法案中。

sentences.en: In the report, ICE tracks and publishes detailed information on the state of immigration enforcement, including: the number of people detained, where they’re being held and for how long, and how many have been deported.
sentences.zh: 在报告中，ICE 跟踪并发布了有关移民执法状况的详细信息，包括：被拘留的人数、被拘留的地点、拘留的时间以及有多少人被驱逐出境。

blocks.html_en: For the past six years, ICE has largely complied with the congressional data-reporting mandate. There were short delays on occasion, including last fall during a prolonged government shutdown — but the current gap is the longest, experts say.

sentences.en: For the past six years, ICE has largely complied with the congressional data-reporting mandate.

sentences.en: There were short delays on occasion, including last fall during a prolonged government shutdown — but the current gap is the longest, experts say.
sentences.zh: 专家表示，有时会出现短暂的延误，包括去年秋天政府长期关门期间的延误，但目前的延误是最长的。

blocks.html_en: “It’s important for us to know what our government is doing, especially in the Trump administration, which is known for its fabulism,” said Aaron Reichlin-Melnick, senior fellow at the American Immigration Council, a think tank that provides pro bono deportation defense and advocates for immigration reform.

sentences.zh: 美国移民委员会高级研究员亚伦·赖克林-梅尔尼克表示：“了解我们的政府在做什么对我们来说很重要，尤其是在以谎言闻名的特朗普政府。”美国移民委员会是一家提供无偿驱逐辩护并倡导移民改革的智库。

blocks.html_en: Deportation flights, tracked independently by the nonprofit Human Rights First, continue to rise. But without official statistics, experts say, the full scope and consequences of the mass deportation program are impossible to know.

sentences.en: Deportation flights, tracked independently by the nonprofit Human Rights First, continue to rise.
sentences.zh: 由非营利组织“人权第一”独立追踪的驱逐航班数量继续增加。

sentences.en: But without official statistics, experts say, the full scope and consequences of the mass deportation program are impossible to know.
sentences.zh: 但专家表示，如果没有官方统计数据，就不可能了解大规模驱逐计划的全部范围和后果。

blocks.html_en: Because the transparency requirement was embedded in an appropriations bill, there is some debate about whether ICE is still legally required to report the data, Reichlin-Melnick said. The government has been operating since last year with funding approved under a process known as “reconciliation,” which Republicans could pass with a simple majority vote.

sentences.zh: Reichlin-Melnick 表示，由于透明度要求已包含在拨款法案中，因此对于法律上是否仍要求 ICE 报告数据存在一些争议。

sentences.en: The government has been operating since last year with funding approved under a process known as “reconciliation,” which Republicans could pass with a simple majority vote.
sentences.zh: 自去年以来，政府一直在根据“和解”程序批准资金运作，共和党人可以以简单多数票通过该程序。

blocks.html_en: Under two separate reconciliation bills, Congress has given the Department of Homeland Security more than $240 billion for immigration enforcement through fiscal year 2029 — more money than the department has ever had. The first tranche of funding included $45 billion specifically for ICE detention.

sentences.en: Under two separate reconciliation bills, Congress has given the Department of Homeland Security more than $240 billion for immigration enforcement through fiscal year 2029 — more money than the department has ever had.
sentences.zh: 根据两项单独的和解法案，国会已向国土安全部拨款超过 2,400 亿美元，用于截至 2029 财年的移民执法——比该部以往拥有的资金还要多。

sentences.en: The first tranche of funding included $45 billion specifically for ICE detention.
sentences.zh: 第一笔资金包括专门用于 ICE 拘留的 450 亿美元。

blocks.html_en: For ICE to claim at any point since then that they were lacking funds “is not only disingenuous; it’s counter-factual,” said Heidi Altman, vice president of policy at the National Immigration Law Center, which advocates for the rights of low-income immigrants.

sentences.en: For ICE to claim at any point since then that they were lacking funds “is not only disingenuous; it’s counter-factual,” said Heidi Altman, vice president of policy at the National Immigration Law Center, which advocates for the rights of low-income immigrants.
sentences.zh: 倡导低收入移民权利的国家移民法律中心政策副总裁海蒂·奥尔特曼表示，从那时起，ICE 在任何时候声称他们缺乏资金“不仅是不诚实的，而且是反事实的”。

blocks.html_en: “As part of good governance, ICE should be reporting this basic data,” she said. “It shouldn’t require congressional instructions to do so.”

sentences.en: “As part of good governance, ICE should be reporting this basic data,” she said.
sentences.zh: “作为良好治理的一部分，ICE 应该报告这些基本数据，”她说。

sentences.en: “It shouldn’t require congressional instructions to do so.”
sentences.zh: “这样做不应该需要国会的指示。”

blocks.html_en: Other Homeland Security agencies have continued to provide timely information. Customs and Border Protection has updated its dashboard of migrant encounters through May, and ICE itself has continued to publish new 287g agreements through May.

sentences.en: Other Homeland Security agencies have continued to provide timely information.
sentences.zh: 其他国土安全机构继续提供及时信息。

sentences.en: Customs and Border Protection has updated its dashboard of migrant encounters through May, and ICE itself has continued to publish new 287g agreements through May.
sentences.zh: 海关和边境保护局已更新其 5 月份遇到的移民情况仪表板，ICE 本身也在 5 月份继续发布新的 287g 协议。

blocks.html_en: Public data is “a form of accountability,” Vaughan said. “Voters are interested in this, and they’re going to be making decisions in many cases on the president’s record on immigration.”

sentences.en: Public data is “a form of accountability,” Vaughan said.
sentences.zh: 沃恩说，公共数据是“一种问责形式”。

sentences.en: “Voters are interested in this, and they’re going to be making decisions in many cases on the president’s record on immigration.”
sentences.zh: “选民对此很感兴趣，在很多情况下他们将根据总统在移民问题上的记录做出决定。”

content_html_zh:
过去一周发生的两名移民被移民和海关执法人员枪杀事件，让特朗普政府的暴力镇压行为再次暴露无遗，引发抗议和追究责任。 但自四月份以来，政府一直对参与执法行动的总人数保密，这激怒了保守派和进步派。

自特朗普上任以来，已有数十名移民在 ICE 拘留期间死亡。 最近几天，Lorenzo Salgado Araujo 和 Joan Sebastian Guerrero 两人被 ICE 特工枪杀。 专家表示，在中期选举年，迫切需要提高透明度。 政治右翼人士希望证明总统正在兑现驱逐数百万人的承诺，而左翼人士则想知道移民社区受到了多少损害。

主张限制合法移民的移民研究中心政策研究主任杰西卡·沃恩表示：“即使有充分的理由不公开这些信息，但这仍然给人留下他们试图隐瞒某些事情的印象。” “他们应该担心给人留下这样的印象，因为他们已经受到如此多的审查。”

自 4 月 9 日该机构报告拘留了 6 万多人以来，ICE 就没有发布过关于逮捕、拘留和驱逐出境的半月刊“拘留管理”报告。

新闻媒体、智囊团和学术界已要求提供缺失的数据，但 ICE 拒绝提供这些数据，引发了至少三起不同的诉讼，其中包括一个极右翼组织发起的诉讼，要求提供“真实的驱逐统计数据”。

美国国土安全部在一封电子邮件中告诉《马歇尔计划》，自特朗普 2025 年 1 月上任以来，截至 6 月 24 日，政府已逮捕了超过 981,000 人，并驱逐了 948,000 人。

ICE 发言人将数据发布的延迟归咎于一系列政府关闭。 该发言人在一封电子邮件中写道：“ICE 正在努力研究该报告[原文如此]并尽快发布相应数据。”

过去长达数月的关闭有时会减慢数据的发布速度，但“政府已经开放了一段时间，这些报告应该是例行公事，”沃恩说。

国土安全部还表示，“超过 300 万非法移民已离开美国…… 其中包括特朗普重新执政第一年估计有 220 万人自我驱逐。 该部门尚未透露如何定义或追踪所谓的自我驱逐出境，也没有说明该数字的来源。

“我想每个人都知道，通过新闻稿和社交媒体传播的数字并不完全正确，”右翼公共政策非营利组织“监督项目”的主任迈克·豪威尔在与前特朗普顾问史蒂夫·班农的播客中表示。

监督项目本月早些时候就移民执法数据起诉特朗普政府，寻求支持政府主张的记录。

豪厄尔的初创公司是保守派传统基金会的分支机构，加入了寻求记录的新闻和非营利组织的名单。

加州大学伯克利分校和加州大学洛杉矶分校的驱逐数据项目发布了从 ICE 收到的移民数据，该项目也起诉了政府；马歇尔项目已提交一份非当事人意见陈述以支持投诉。 该诉讼寻求记录“提供一个定量窗口，了解美国政府如何执行移民执法以及与过去政府的比较。”

驱逐数据项目已成为记者和研究人员的首选来源，该项目建立在交易记录访问信息交换所等组织汇编移民数据的工作基础上。 它提出《信息自由法》请求，在机构拖延时提起诉讼，并免费将原始文件发布到网上，并提供过滤到特定州或大致地区的工具。 透明度倡导者表示，这项工作不应免除 ICE 公开报告其数据的责任。

自 2019 年以来，国会要求 ICE 每两周发布一份报告，公布详细的逮捕、拘留和驱逐数据。 该任务在两党支持下被纳入 2020 年国土安全部拨款法案中。

该法规很明确：ICE 主管“应在可公开访问的网站上以可下载、可搜索和可排序的格式提供一份报告”，每半月更新一次。 在报告中，ICE 跟踪并发布了有关移民执法状况的详细信息，包括：被拘留的人数、被拘留的地点、拘留的时间以及有多少人被驱逐出境。

在过去六年中，ICE 基本上遵守了国会的数据报告授权。 专家表示，有时会出现短暂的延误，包括去年秋天政府长期关门期间的延误，但目前的延误是最长的。

美国移民委员会高级研究员亚伦·赖克林-梅尔尼克表示：“了解我们的政府在做什么对我们来说很重要，尤其是在以谎言闻名的特朗普政府。”美国移民委员会是一家提供无偿驱逐辩护并倡导移民改革的智库。

由非营利组织“人权第一”独立追踪的驱逐航班数量继续增加。 但专家表示，如果没有官方统计数据，就不可能了解大规模驱逐计划的全部范围和后果。

Reichlin-Melnick 表示，由于透明度要求已包含在拨款法案中，因此对于法律上是否仍要求 ICE 报告数据存在一些争议。 自去年以来，政府一直在根据“和解”程序批准资金运作，共和党人可以以简单多数票通过该程序。

根据两项单独的和解法案，国会已向国土安全部拨款超过 2,400 亿美元，用于截至 2029 财年的移民执法——比该部以往拥有的资金还要多。 第一笔资金包括专门用于 ICE 拘留的 450 亿美元。

倡导低收入移民权利的国家移民法律中心政策副总裁海蒂·奥尔特曼表示，从那时起，ICE 在任何时候声称他们缺乏资金“不仅是不诚实的，而且是反事实的”。

“作为良好治理的一部分，ICE 应该报告这些基本数据，”她说。 “这样做不应该需要国会的指示。”

其他国土安全机构继续提供及时信息。 海关和边境保护局已更新其 5 月份遇到的移民情况仪表板，ICE 本身也在 5 月份继续发布新的 287g 协议。

沃恩说，公共数据是“一种问责形式”。 “选民对此很感兴趣，在很多情况下他们将根据总统在移民问题上的记录做出决定。”
